findings.title: Org guard runs after task fetch in recommend_skills
findings.description: The new `!orgId || task.organizationId !== orgId` guard is placed after `stateManager.getTask(taskId)`, meaning an unauthenticated caller (no session org) still triggers a Firestore read and gets a distinguishable `not_found` vs `forbidden` error — leaking task existence. In the same PR, code-review.ts uses the stricter pattern: `requireOrgMatch` runs before any data access. Splitting the guard into `if (!orgId) return forbidden` before the fetch would close the information leak, save the wasted read, and stay consistent with the code-review.ts pattern introduced in this same PR.
